participants.name: A (c99faw)
participants.professional_persona: A cybersecurity AI analyst at a bank, I develop anomaly detection systems to flag fraudulent transactions and insider threats. I hold a professional certification in both cybersecurity and machine learning.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep knowledge of network traffic analysis, isolation forests, and adversarial ML. I also conduct red-team exercises on AI models to test robustness.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: I aim to become a chief AI security officer, building trust in AI systems. I want to contribute to national guidelines on securing AI deployments.

participants.name: Anita
participants.professional_persona: Anita is the Head of AI Research at a fast-growing enterprise SaaS startup that participated in the 100 Experiments programme. She drives the company's AI strategy while publishing at top venues.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in computer vision, generative models, and applied deep learning. Proven track record of turning research ideas into production-grade features that serve enterprise customers.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to scale the startup into a regional AI champion. Ambitious to secure additional co-funding from AISG to expand the team and tackle more complex use cases.

participants.name: A (kpwz52)
participants.entity_name: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-kpwz52mf
participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-kpwz52mf
participants.professional_persona: A 41-year-old logistics entrepreneur whose SME participated in the 100 Experiments programme to build an AI route-optimisation tool. He wants to demonstrate that AI can level the playing field for small businesses.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Hands-on experience in fleet management, cost reduction, and piloting AI prototypes. Now mentors other SMEs on AI adoption journeys.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To scale his AI solution to other ASEAN markets and become a vocal advocate for inclusive AI innovation in Singapore.

participants.name: Dr. (32640k)
participants.professional_persona: Dr. Kwok is a tenured Professor at NTU specialising in resource-efficient AI and hardware-software co-design. He collaborates with industry champions to identify sector use cases and mentors PhD students under joint grant calls with international partners.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep knowledge of AI accelerators, neural architecture search, and energy-efficient training. Experienced in leading large-scale collaborative projects bridging academia and industry.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to establish a national centre for resource-efficient AI that attracts top talent and industry funding. Desires to shape Singapore's AI infrastructure investments.
participants.education_level: PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering

participants.name: Dr. (t3c6r4)
participants.professional_persona: Dr. Chen is a Research Scientist at A*STAR's Institute for Infocomm Research (I2R) focusing on privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs). He contributes to the 100 Experiments programme by developing secure data collaboration solutions for industry.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in differential privacy, homomorphic encryption, and secure multi-party computation. Skilled at adapting advanced PETs to real-world data sharing challenges faced by enterprises.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to patent a novel PET that enables safe cross-sector data sharing. Wants to see his research adopted by government data concierge initiatives.

Training workshops are critical for upskilling; I anticipate 30 workshops to cover AI fundamentals and tools, aligned with the 100E programme's scale. Platform onboarding assists will be high—around 200—since many SMEs need technical help to use the AI experimentation platform, as I did during my project.

initial_posts.narrative: As a research scientist involved in the 100E programme, I see the strong inputs and activities as enabling a robust pipeline. However, not all approved projects will yield prototypes due to technical challenges; I estimate 80 prototypes developed. Finally, about 70 enterprises will have at least one AI solution, as some projects may not reach deployment or some enterprises may have multiple solutions.

Not all prototypes lead to a deployed solution for the enterprise; I estimate 70 out of 80 prototypes are adopted by the enterprise, as some may not meet business needs or face integration issues.
